question_text: Why does the Southern Hemisphere experience winter when the Northern Hemisphere experiences summer?
correct_answer: Because the Southern Hemisphere is tilted away from the Sun while the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards it.
explanation: Earth's axis is tilted in one direction. When the Northern Hemisphere is tilted towards the Sun (summer), the Southern Hemisphere is simultaneously tilted away from the Sun (winter). As Earth orbits, this tilt remains in the same direction, so the hemispheres experience opposite seasons.
